mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with FEDFUNDS changes (Rank IC < 0.02) across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 7.5%, labor market deterioration signals impending recessionary pressures that force equity markets to price in lower corporate earnings over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns remain positive in over 55% of instances when UNRATE crosses above 7.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real personal income and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer spending and construction activity, driving positive equity returns as aggregate demand strengthens.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS and HOUST rise together, with out-of-sample accuracy below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and output utilization, which translates into immediate positive market sentiment and short-term S&P 500 gains.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases consistently coincide with negative 5-day forward returns during early-cycle phases, invalidating the supply-demand transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly gains in total nonfarm payrolls directly increase household income and consumption capacity, creating a persistent upward bias in equity valuations over the following month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero correlation with 20-day forward returns across multiple rolling windows, with Rank IC consistently below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price inflation above 3.0% forces central banks to tighten policy expectations, raising discount rates and compressing equity multiples over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Equity markets deliver positive 60-day returns in over 60% of periods following CPIAUCSL inflation breaches, negating the rate-hike transmission mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly GDP expansion and declining unemployment rates confirm robust economic momentum, driving institutional capital allocation into equities and generating positive forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined GDPC1 and UNRATE signal produces negative forward returns in majority of observations, breaking the growth-employment linkage.

mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates alongside slowing payroll growth indicate stagflationary pressures that erode corporate profit margins and trigger immediate equity sell-offs.
failure_condition: The FEDFUNDS-PAYEMS divergence fails to predict negative returns, with market performance remaining neutral or positive during stagflationary episodes.
